title: Drammen HK

infobox.value: Leaving   * [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") [Emil Hansson](/wiki/Emil_Hansson_(handballer,_born_1996) "Emil Hansson (handballer, born 1996)") (RW) to [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") [HF Karlskrona](/wiki/HF_Karlskrona "HF Karlskrona") * [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") [August Olsen Storbugt](/w/index.php?title=August_Olsen_Storbugt&action=edit&redlink=1 "August Olsen Storbugt (page does not exist)") (LP) to [Denmark](/wiki/Denmark "Denmark") [Nordsjælland Håndbold](/wiki/Nordsj%C3%A6lland_H%C3%A5ndbold "Nordsjælland Håndbold")

is a [Norwegian](/wiki/Norway "Norway") [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") team located in [Drammen](/wiki/Drammen "Drammen"), [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). They compete in [REMA 1000-ligaen](/wiki/Eliteserien_(men%27s_handball) "Eliteserien (men's handball)"), which is the highest league for men's handball clubs in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway").

Norwegian handball club

**Drammen Håndballklubb** is a [Norwegian](/wiki/Norway "Norway") [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") team located in [Drammen](/wiki/Drammen "Drammen"), [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). They compete in [REMA 1000-ligaen](/wiki/Eliteserien_(men%27s_handball) "Eliteserien (men's handball)"), which is the highest league for men's handball clubs in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway").

[![Location of Drammen HK](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/28/Norway_location_map.svg/330px-Norway_location_map.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Norway_location_map.svg "Location of Drammen HK")

![Drammen](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Red_pog.svg/20px-Red_pog.svg.png)

Drammen

Location of Drammen HK

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Drammen_HK&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club was founded on March 3, 1992.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The foundation was the result of a long going project where handball clubs in [Drammen](/wiki/Drammen "Drammen") and [Lier](/wiki/Lier,_Norway "Lier, Norway") gathered to discuss how to build a "Top Team" candidate for the region. The project resulted in two clubs joining forces - Reistad IL from Lier and [IF Sturla](/wiki/IF_Sturla "IF Sturla") from Drammen. The two clubs transferred their senior players to the new club, but continued as separate entities concentrating on development of children and youngsters. The colors of Drammen HK became blue - the same color as background of the city coat of arms.

Reistad IL was qualified to play in Norway Premier Division, so Drammen really got a flying start starting at the top level. However, Drammen did not make it through and was degraded to the Second Division the year after. However, In 1993 Drammen HK signed the Swedish trainer [Kent-Harry Andersson](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kent-Harry_Andersson "de:Kent-Harry Andersson"), and returned to the Premier Division after one year. This was the beginning of a series of successes that was crowned in 1996, bringing home the [EHF City Cup](/wiki/EHF_Challenge_Cup "EHF Challenge Cup") as the first Norwegian club ever to win a European title. In 1997, the team won the silver medal at the first [club world championship](/wiki/IHF_Super_Globe "IHF Super Globe"), after losing 30-29 to the Spanish team [Caja Cantabria](/wiki/CB_Cantabria "CB Cantabria") in the final.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 2007, the team again reached the finals of European Cup Series No. 3 ([Challenge Cup (EHF City Cup)](/wiki/EHF_Challenge_Cup "EHF Challenge Cup"), but lost there against the Romanian [CS UCM Reșița](/wiki/CS_Cara%C8%99_-_Severin_Re%C8%99i%C8%9Ba "CS Caraș - Severin Reșița") team. During its history, it won 4 [Norwegian League](/wiki/Eliteserien_(men%27s_handball) "Eliteserien (men's handball)") (1997, 2007, 2008, 2010), 2 [Norwegian Cup](/wiki/Norwegian_Men%27s_Handball_Cup "Norwegian Men's Handball Cup") (2008, 2017). Some of the best Norway international players started their senior careers at Drammen HK, such as [Glenn Solberg](/wiki/Glenn_Solberg "Glenn Solberg"), [Frode Hagen](/wiki/Frode_Hagen "Frode Hagen") and [Kristian Kjelling](/wiki/Kristian_Kjelling "Kristian Kjelling").
